sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Let me trace the changes:
1. The orange diamond with magenta center moved from (13-17, 29-33) to (16-20, 32-36), now at center (18, 34)
2. The dark gray diamond at (7-11, 47-51) is gone - it was the activated white diamond. Now there's a dark gray diamond at (14-18, 36-40) with orange center (16, 38)
3. The purple ring became a white diamond with orange center at (16-20, 55-59)
4. The dark gray diamond at (19-23, 6-10) with orange center (21, 8) is still there
5. The purple square with magenta center at (39-43, 47-51) is still there
6. The dark gray diamond at (33-37, 43-47) with purple center (35, 45) is still there
7. The dark gray diamond at (46-50, 52-56) with purple center (48, 54) is still there
8. The hollow orange diamond at bottom is still there
So clicking the purple ring again caused:
- The orange diamond to move from (15, 31) to (18, 34) - moved right along the trail
- The white diamond (activated dark gray) at (9, 49) disappeared and a dark gray diamond appeared at (16, 38)
- The purple ring became a white diamond with orange center again
Wait, I notice the white trail changed. There's now a white trail at (18, 39-54) connecting the orange diamond to the white diamond at the ring position. And another white trail at (19, 22-29) and (20, 13-21).
So the pattern seems to be:
1. Click ring → orange diamond moves along trail, ring becomes white diamond
2. Click orange diamond → it moves, previous position becomes white diamond, ring reappears
3. Click dark gray diamond → activates it (turns white)
4. Click ring → orange diamond moves again, ring becomes white diamond
5. Continue...
Now I need to click the orange diamond again to move it, then activate another dark gray diamond.
The orange diamond is now at (18, 34). Let me click it to move it further along the trail.
